jokes.text: When Andy proposed to me, he gave me a ring pop. But then he ate it first.
jokes.setup: Andy gave April a ring pop for proposal - unconventional but sweet
jokes.punchline: He ate it first, making it even more absurd
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - perfectly captures Andy's lovable stupidity and April's deadpan delivery

jokes.text: In 1832, Ben's great-great-great-great grandfather, Teodore Wyatt, a bastard, met a beautiful seamstress from Antwerp.
jokes.setup: Leslie starting to tell the engagement story
jokes.punchline: Goes back to 1832 with unnecessary genealogical detail
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie over-explaining everything with historical context

jokes.text: I've been telling everybody about it. I'm so happy I want to shout it from the rooftops. And she has. We've gotten several noise complaints.
jokes.setup: Leslie wants to metaphorically shout from rooftops
jokes.punchline: Ben reveals she literally did this, getting noise complaints
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of common expression with Leslie taking it literally

jokes.text: The joy that I am feeling right now is profound and unmatched in the modern era. And I can't tell you what it means to me that I am the very first person that you chose to tell.
jokes.setup: Ron expressing deep emotion about being told first
jokes.punchline: Obvious irony since everyone already knows
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron deadpan sarcasm delivered with excessive formality

jokes.text: We have something very exciting to tell you that you have never heard before. Ben and I are engaged. And you're hearing about it for the very first time right now.
jokes.setup: Leslie emphasizing this is brand new information
jokes.punchline: Everyone obviously already knows
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe moment but fairly predictable

jokes.text: Surprisingly, no. Larry Bird has not RSVP'd yet. Well, he kind of waits until the last minute. That's his style. Put him down as a 'maybe'.
jokes.setup: Ben asking about 'you-know-who' responding
jokes.punchline: It's basketball legend Larry Bird, treated casually
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Leslie moment treating celebrity like personal friend

jokes.text: One, put Twizzlers on the candy table. And, two, cancel the party.
jokes.setup: Ben has two small requests
jokes.punchline: First trivial, second massive
jokes.explanation: Great escalation structure with perfect economy

jokes.text: 'No' to canceling the party, and 'no' to Twizzlers. We are a Red Vines family. You're going to have to get used to that.
jokes.setup: Leslie rejecting Ben's requests
jokes.punchline: Treating candy preference as serious family tradition
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's intensity about trivial things

jokes.text: Well, if there's anyone who can bring my parents together, it's no one. No one can ever bring them together.
jokes.setup: Standard 'if anyone can do it...' setup
jokes.punchline: Subverts expectation by saying no one can
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of expected optimistic phrasing

jokes.text: My family situation is complicated. My parents got divorced 30 years ago, and they hate each other. Okay, I guess, it's not that complicated.
jokes.setup: Ben calling his situation complicated
jokes.punchline: Realizes it's actually quite simple
jokes.explanation: Nice self-aware moment with good timing

jokes.text: You guys pretend you're Ron Swanson, and you're trying to decide whether you want to invest in this company. Be super critical. You're short.
jokes.setup: Tom asking for feedback as if they're Ron
jokes.punchline: Adds random physical insult about height
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment but fairly weak punchline

jokes.text: Parents, are you tired of watching your middle-school-aged children grow out of the nice clothes you buy for them? Then rent them from Rent A Swag.
jokes.setup: Business pitch for children's clothes
jokes.punchline: Terrible name 'Rent A Swag'
jokes.explanation: Good Tom/Jean-Ralphio business absurdity

jokes.text: I own it. You rent it. You wear it. You clean it. You return it. I get rich. Wink!
jokes.setup: Explaining the business model
jokes.punchline: Blatantly selfish conclusion with literal 'Wink!'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jean-Ralphio moment with his shameless honesty

jokes.text: It's the greatest thing I've ever seen in my entire life. No. Yes. It's terrible.
jokes.setup: April appearing to love the presentation
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts herself multiple times
jokes.explanation: Perfect April confusion and deadpan delivery

jokes.text: You and I go stargazing in Harvey James Park while my parents slowly strangle each other in the living room.
jokes.setup: Ben suggesting romantic alternative plan
jokes.punchline: Casually describes parents murdering each other
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor with perfect casual delivery

jokes.text: Of all of my metaphorical art projects, this is by far the coziest.
jokes.setup: Leslie describing her unity quilt
jokes.punchline: Implies she has many metaphorical art projects
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie character moment about her over-the-top projects

jokes.text: And, out of respect, I did not include any images of the only other man in the world who's as sexy as you, Joe Biden. Yes, you did, right there. Oh, well, that's just a little one.
jokes.setup: Leslie claiming she left Joe Biden off out of respect
jokes.punchline: Ben points out Biden is clearly on it, Leslie admits it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment with her Biden obsession and inability to resist

jokes.text: My romantic life has been a bit of a mixed bag lately.
jokes.setup: Ann describing her romantic situation
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement given her dating history
jokes.explanation: Decent understatement but not particularly strong

jokes.text: This was literally the most beautiful, and moving thing that I have ever heard. There, there, baby boy. Take all the time you need.
jokes.setup: Chris overcome with emotion from Ann's toast
jokes.punchline: Ann treating him like a crying child
jokes.explanation: Good Chris emotional moment with Ann's maternal response

jokes.text: Well, sure, in a wrestling match, Kirk would win. But, overall, who would you rather have at the helm of your Sovereign-class starship? Jean-Luc Picard. No contest.
jokes.setup: Jerry and Tom having serious Star Trek debate
jokes.punchline: Treating fictional captains as real leadership choice
jokes.explanation: Decent nerd humor but fairly standard

jokes.text: The toast that you gave to Ben and Leslie was so beautiful. And I'm feeling very raw from my therapy. I'm so happy and so sad. It's like a perfect storm of emotions.
jokes.setup: Chris explaining why he's emotional
jokes.punchline: Overwrought description of normal mixed feelings
jokes.explanation: Good Chris character work with his over-analytical emotional state

jokes.text: Oh! There's no more tissue. Everything ends. No, no, no. No, there's more tissue. Everything goes away.
jokes.setup: Chris having emotional breakdown
jokes.punchline: Tissue availability triggers existential crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris moment turning mundane thing into existential crisis

jokes.text: And I said, 'Rules or no rules, I just have to kiss that beautiful face.' Oh, well, that's a Knope woman for you. When we see something adorable, we attack it.
jokes.setup: Leslie telling story about being aggressive with Ben
jokes.punchline: Julia describes Knope women as predatory toward cute things
jokes.explanation: Good family trait humor with predatory language

jokes.text: We didn't know it was spring break.
jokes.setup: Julia seeing Ben's dad with much younger girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Implies girlfriend is college-aged
jokes.explanation: Brutal one-liner with perfect economy and timing

jokes.text: We're a Twizzlers family.
jokes.setup: Leslie offered Red Vines earlier declaring them a Red Vines family
jokes.punchline: Ulani contradicts this, creating candy division
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that adds to family tension

jokes.text: I called a cab. It's outside. I gave the driver $100 to sit and wait. So, whenever we're ready to go, like, maybe, right now, all we have to do is walk outside.
jokes.setup: Ben prepared for escape
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate suggestions to leave immediately
jokes.explanation: Good escalating desperation from Ben

jokes.text: Well, they're white people from Minnesota. So, hockey, fishing, skiing, sailing. And after a few drinks put on a Prince album.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining how to relate to his parents
jokes.punchline: Stereotypical Minnesota interests plus Prince reference
jokes.explanation: Decent regional stereotype humor with Prince twist

jokes.text: Also, I have enough frequent flyer miles to get us to Australia tonight.
jokes.setup: Ben giving practical advice
jokes.punchline: Reveals elaborate escape plan
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ben's desperation

jokes.text: You are a beautiful, talented, brilliant, powerful musk ox. Thank you, ox, for keeping this ship afloat.
jokes.setup: Leslie praising Ann as a good friend
jokes.punchline: Compares her to musk ox and calls her ox
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie moment with her bizarre but loving compliments

jokes.text: Because I'm dating myself right now. Okay? I'm trying to figure out how to make me happy. Ugh. Why is it always about you, Ann? Self-centered much?
jokes.setup: Ann explaining why she's not dating Chris
jokes.punchline: April calls her self-centered for focusing on herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect April logic calling self-care selfish

jokes.text: Ever since I got my first job at the age of nine, I have put all my money into gold, which is currently at an all-time high. So, I have a certain amount of money. I've said too much.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his investment strategy
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's revealed too much about his wealth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment with his secretiveness about wealth

jokes.text: Ricka, ricka, ricka, ricka Ha, ha, ha, ha, ha Swanson Jean-Ralphio. That's me.
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio arriving at the party
jokes.punchline: Makes weird sounds and introduces himself unnecessarily
jokes.explanation: Standard Jean-Ralphio weirdness but not exceptional

jokes.text: I just free-associated all over the moustache.
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio doing word association
jokes.punchline: Describes it in vaguely sexual terms to Ron
jokes.explanation: Good Jean-Ralphio inappropriate phrasing

jokes.text: Okay. Rethink that move, son.
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio's inappropriate comment
jokes.punchline: Ron's understated warning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron reaction to inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: In ninth grade, a quilt ended an argument between my two best friends. And a quilt settled the Donna-Jerry parking lot feud of 2006. What was the third time? Right now.
jokes.setup: Leslie claiming three quilt successes
jokes.punchline: Third time is the current situation she's trying to fix
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie confidence despite current failure

jokes.text: Police, open up, we have a warrant! Just kidding, it's us. Ha! We got you.
jokes.setup: Sounds like police raid
jokes.punchline: Andy's fake-out joke at engagement party
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy inappropriate humor at wrong time

jokes.text: You are such a brave, good dog with so much spirit. You have overcome so much. You are the most wonderful dog in the whole world. And I am so happy for you.
jokes.setup: Chris greeting Champion the dog
jokes.punchline: Overly emotional speech to a dog as if it's a person
jokes.explanation: Good Chris emotional excess applied to a dog

jokes.text: Not technically an adult.
jokes.setup: Ulani asking why she's not on the quilt
jokes.punchline: Leslie implies she's not an adult due to age
jokes.explanation: Sharp dig at the age gap with perfect timing

jokes.text: See, Ben? They're agreeing. It's working.
jokes.setup: Parents both calling quilt a disaster
jokes.punchline: Leslie sees their mutual hatred as agreement/success
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie optimism turning disaster into success

jokes.text: But sometimes you've got to work a little, so you can ball a lot.
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio giving life advice about work
jokes.punchline: Uses 'ball' as verb meaning party/live luxuriously
jokes.explanation: Good Jean-Ralphio philosophy with his unique phrasing

jokes.text: Pizza, the beach, rock and roll music. Smallpox, botched surgery, snails crawling out of your mouth. This is weirdly working. It's evening me out.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to balance Chris's emotions with happy/sad things
jokes.punchline: Bizarre therapy actually working
jokes.explanation: Great absurd therapy concept with good escalation

jokes.text: She's ruining your quilt, just like she ruined our marriage.
jokes.setup: Julia cutting the quilt
jokes.punchline: Stephen relates it to their failed marriage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of petty argument to marriage failure

jokes.text: You're what? Oh, boy. I didn't mean to steal your spotlight, but the cat's out of the bag. Young Ben is going to have a new baby sister.
jokes.setup: Ulani reveals pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Stephen proudly announces it at Ben's engagement party
jokes.explanation: Perfect inappropriate announcement at wrong time

jokes.text: Still firing bullets, son.
jokes.setup: Stephen announcing girlfriend's pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Crude reference to his sexual potency
jokes.explanation: Appropriately inappropriate dad comment

jokes.text: It's like the War of the Wyatts out there, huh?
jokes.setup: Marlene commenting on family fighting
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'War of the Roses' with family name
jokes.explanation: Decent pun but fairly obvious wordplay

jokes.text: Sometimes, he won the argument, and sometimes I won. But usually, we forgot what we were arguing about and just had sex.
jokes.setup: Marlene explaining how she and Leslie's dad resolved fights
jokes.punchline: Too much information about their sex life
jokes.explanation: Standard parent oversharing humor

jokes.text: How can you be so wise and so inappropriate at the same time? I'm a parent.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking how her mom combines wisdom with oversharing
jokes.punchline: Her mom's simple explanation that it comes with parenthood
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about parental paradoxes with a clean, economical punchline.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, no, I'm definitely not.
jokes.setup: Tom asking if he's serious about the business
jokes.punchline: Jean-Ralphio's completely honest admission of his lack of commitment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jean-Ralphio honesty. His complete lack of business sense delivered matter-of-factly is character gold.

jokes.text: I grabbed all of the brownies from the dessert table and four bottles of wine. Get in the car. We're going to Australia.
jokes.setup: Ben suggesting escape
jokes.punchline: Has already stolen supplies and planned international flight
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ben's desperation with callback

jokes.text: For another $100, you can do whatever you want.
jokes.setup: Leslie worried about making out in cab
jokes.punchline: Driver matter-of-factly offers to allow anything for money
jokes.explanation: Decent one-liner but not exceptional

jokes.text: Ann Perkins. You are, without a doubt, the queen of toilet humor. That's all I ever wanted to be.
jokes.setup: Chris praising Ann for bathroom joke
jokes.punchline: Ann's mock pride in lowbrow humor achievement
jokes.explanation: Good Ann/Chris moment with self-aware humor

jokes.text: You can either take it, or you can take it.
jokes.setup: Leslie giving ultimatum to Ben's parents
jokes.punchline: No actual choice - both options identical
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie take-charge moment with great economy

jokes.text: Clubs. Girls. Dancing. Naked. Mom? Argument. Police. Fleeing the scene. Hiding in a dumpster. Coming here. Crashing on your couch for a week because, technically, I'm homeless.
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio describing his night out
jokes.punchline: Escalates from fun to complete disaster and homelessness
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating disaster story in rapid-fire delivery
